text: Run this Python script and tell me the output
notes: Assumes script is provided or attached; straightforward run_python call

text: Execute this SQL query against the database: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending'
notes: Explicit SQL execution request with query provided

text: Check this JavaScript code for any linting errors
notes: lint_code should detect language from context (JavaScript) even if not explicitly typed

text: Explain what this function does
notes: Direct explain_code call; assumes code is attached or in context

text: Can you run this Python snippet for me? `print([x**2 for x in range(10)])`
notes: Inline Python code snippet provided directly in the instruction

text: What does this code do? Walk me through it line by line
notes: explain_code with detailed/verbose mode expected

text: Run a SQL query to get the top 10 customers by revenue
notes: Agent needs to construct the SQL query; assumes access to a database schema

text: Lint my Python file before I push it to production
notes: lint_code for Python; assumes file is provided or accessible

text: Execute SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE created_at > '2024-01-01'
notes: Direct SQL execution with full query provided

text: I don't understand what this recursive algorithm does. Can you break it down for me?
notes: explain_code with focus on recursion pattern; code assumed to be provided

text: Check this TypeScript file for any style violations or errors
notes: lint_code for TypeScript; language explicitly mentioned

text: Run the script and tell me if there are any runtime errors
notes: Context implies Python script; run_python with error capture

text: Just run it and give me the result
notes: Casual instruction assuming code is already provided/uploaded; default to run_python

text: Lint this Python file first, then run it if there are no errors
notes: Conditional chain: lint_code first; only run_python if lint passes

text: Explain what this SQL query does and then run it
notes: Sequential: explain_code first for understanding, then run_sql for results

text: Check this code for issues and explain any problems you find
notes: lint_code detects issues; explain_code explains the nature of those issues

text: Run this script and if it throws an exception, explain what went wrong
notes: Conditional: run_python first; if error occurs, call explain_code on the traceback

text: Run this SQL query to get all failed transactions, then write a Python script to analyze the results
notes: run_sql output feeds into run_python; Python script uses the query result data

text: Look at this code, explain what it does, then lint it for any issues
notes: Sequential: explain first, then lint for code quality analysis

text: Run this Python data processing script and then execute a SQL query to store the output in the database
notes: run_python first to generate data; run_sql uses output as INSERT data

text: Lint the code, fix any issues, then run it
notes: lint_code identifies issues; agent applies fixes then calls run_python

text: Explain this regex pattern to me and then run a Python test to verify it works on sample inputs
notes: explain_code for regex comprehension; run_python to validate with test cases

text: Run a SQL query to find all duplicate emails, then write a cleanup script in Python
notes: run_sql identifies duplicates; run_python uses that data to perform cleanup logic

text: Check the syntax of this code and tell me what it's supposed to accomplish
notes: Parallel-ish but natural sequential: lint for syntax, then explain purpose

text: Execute this Python benchmark script and then run the SQL query to log the performance metrics
notes: run_python for benchmarking; run_sql logs results to a metrics table

text: Run the code
notes: No code provided; should ask for the code. Also unclear what language to run

text: Execute this against the database
notes: Missing the actual query; should prompt user for the SQL to execute

text: Explain the algorithm
notes: No code attached; agent should ask 'which algorithm?' or request code to explain

text: Check my code for errors
notes: Missing: actual code content. Could also be ambiguous between lint_code and explain_code

text: Run a query to pull our sales data
notes: Vague: no table name, date range, columns, or filters specified. Needs schema context

text: Debug this for me
notes: No code provided; 'debug' maps ambiguously to lint_code, run_python, or explain_code

text: Can you execute this script?
notes: No script attached; unclear language. Should prompt user to share the code

text: Validate the code before deploying
notes: 'Validate' is ambiguous: lint? run tests? explain? No code provided either

text: Run the analysis
notes: Completely unspecified: no script, no SQL, no context about what analysis to run

text: What does this do?
notes: No code provided; tool is inferable (explain_code) but subject is missing

text: Check the function I wrote earlier
notes: Relies on conversational context that may not be present; 'check' is also ambiguous

text: Query the users table
notes: Table is specified but no columns, filters, or purpose; needs clarification on what to retrieve

text: Run the tests
notes: No test file or test suite specified; could also mean running a SQL validation script

text: Lint my Python file and also explain the main function in parallel
notes: Both lint_code and explain_code run simultaneously on the same file

text: Run this Python script and at the same time execute this SQL query to check the current DB state
notes: Parallel execution explicitly requested; two independent operations

text: Check both my Python file and my SQL query for errors simultaneously
notes: Two parallel lint_code calls for different code artifacts

text: Explain this Python class and also explain this SQL stored procedure
notes: Two parallel explain_code calls for different code artifacts

text: Run the ETL script and also execute the validation SQL query at the same time
notes: ETL and validation are independent; parallel execution is safe and efficient

text: Lint all three of my Python modules at once
notes: Three parallel lint_code calls for three separate modules

text: Run the data ingestion script and check for any linting issues while it's running
notes: Parallel: run_python for execution, lint_code for static analysis simultaneously

text: Explain this code and lint it at the same time — I'm in a hurry
notes: Explicit parallel request; both tools applied to same code

text: Run the Python analysis and the SQL aggregation report at the same time and give me both outputs
notes: Parallel execution; results from both returned together

text: Can you lint the frontend and backend code simultaneously?
notes: Two parallel lint_code calls; frontend likely JS/TS, backend likely Python

text: Run the unit tests and explain the test coverage report at the same time
notes: Parallel: run_python for test execution, explain_code for coverage output analysis

text: Execute the data cleanup SQL and run the Python report generator while that's happening
notes: Both operations are independent and explicitly requested to run concurrently
